title: Project Overview & Entry Point
description: Start with the onboarding guide and entry point to understand what this project is — a Svelte 5 damage calculator for the Roblox game Voxel Blade. The HTML shell loads main.ts, which mounts the root App component.

title: Core Type System
description: Before diving into logic, understand the shared type definitions that every other module depends on. types.ts defines the stat system (StatKey, StatMap), character entities (Race, Guild), equipment shapes (Armor, Ring, Rune), weapon components, and BuildState — the central state object. dmgTypes.ts extends this with computed damage type interfaces.

title: Game Constants & Balance Numbers
description: The constants layer holds every tunable number in the game: perk scaling formulas, crit damage formulas, damage type metadata with colors, debuff combat values, HP formulas, cooldown reduction, defensive DR, DoT formulas, draconic element multipliers, and healing constants. These are consumed by the engine and data layers.

title: JSON Game Data
description: All game items are defined in JSON data files: armors (100+ sets with shared parts and per-slot stats), weapon blades/handles/gloves/essences (each with tier, attack speed, damage type weights, scalings, perks), rings, runes with cooldowns, enchantments with stat modifiers, guilds with rank progression, races with passives, and 300+ perks with tag classifications.

title: Engine Data Access Layer
description: The engine's data modules load JSON files at module init, build name-keyed lookup maps, and export typed accessor functions (getRace, getGuild, getBlade, getHandle, getEnchant, getRing, getRune, etc.) used by all calculation code. The barrel module data.ts re-exports everything through a single import path.

title: Build Calculation Pipeline
description: The core calculation engine. calcBuild orchestrates the full pipeline: accumulateEquipment collects stats/perks from all slots (race, guild, armor, ring, rune, weapon, infusions), finalizePerks applies effectiveness/cursed/potency post-processing, then deriveResults computes CDR, crit, boosts, and buff states. This is the heart of the damage calculator.

title: Critical Hit & Defense Systems
description: Two critical subsystems: crit.ts aggregates 15+ natural and extra crit sources (dexterity, elemental boosts, perks like Flowing Crits and Perfection), applies Primal divisor logic, and computes effective crit chance/damage. defense.ts resolves percent and flat defense sources into damage multipliers and maps damage types to applicable defense stat keys via DEF_GROUP.

title: Damage Type Resolution & Bonuses
description: Damage type resolution handles merging base types with bonuses, Fire↔Air bidirectional conversion (Echo Incineration), weapon-art type detection (same-as-weapon, highest-type modes), and Air→Magic conversion with Dark Magic hex. dmgTypeBonuses computes elemental damage bonuses from perks like Void Rage and Draconic Blood.

title: Buff & Debuff Data System
description: The buff system is the largest data module. BuffData.ts defines 30+ buff/debuff entries, granted-buff factories for perks/runes/weapon-arts/races, the complete buff assembly pipeline (item buffs → perk buffs → weapon-art buffs → modifiers → Tailwind→Whirlwind conversion → Cauterize burn mode), and potency modifiers. Buffs drive many conditional damage boosts.

title: Perk-Based Damage & DoT Engines
description: Perkbasedmg.ts is the declarative registry of 30+ perk damage definitions with base formulas, damage types, scalings, hit counts, proc coefficients, secondary effects, and HP gates. DoTDamage.ts computes Bleed/Burn/Poison tick damage with potency scaling and Caustic Slow. TypedDmgBoost.ts calculates per-damage-type multipliers from active perks.

title: Weapon Arts, Mount Runes & Draconic Effects
description: Weapon arts define 48 special attacks with damage formulas, cooldowns, hit structures, and equip requirements. Weaponartcheck.ts validates whether a weapon art can be equipped. Mount runes define mount-specific M1/weapon-art attacks. Draconic systems include color-based stat bonuses, draconic rune/infusion damage bonuses, and dragon state mechanics.

title: State Management & Persistence
description: The Svelte store manages the application's BuildState with debounced localStorage persistence. A derived store runs calcBuild on every state change to produce live results. Exported functions handle enchantment swapping, guild setting (with Draconic special cases), armor slot movement, and ring-infusion swapping.

title: Root App Component & Damage Analyzer
description: App.svelte (~4000 lines) is the main orchestrator managing all top-level state, modal systems, drag-and-drop, stat/tag filtering, and child component coordination. DamageAnalyzer.svelte (~6700 lines) is the largest component, wiring together weapon stats, buff aggregation, defensive perks, elemental bonuses, DoT, and self-damage into the full damage analysis UI.

title: UI Component Library & Design System
description: A Svelte 5 design system with primitives (Badge, Button, Card, Section, Panel, Stat, Divider), reusable modals (RaceModal, GuildModal, AccessorySelectModal, WeaponPartModal), filter components (TagFilter, StatFilter, WeaponStatFilter with tri-state chips), search components (SuggestDrop, DidYouMean, Highlight), and the global CSS design tokens in app.css.

title: Build Configuration & Deployment
description: The project uses Vite with Svelte and PWA plugins, TypeScript with strict configs for both app and node code, Vercel for deployment with PWA caching headers, and GitHub Actions for CI/CD to GitHub Pages. Package.json defines scripts for dev, build, preview, and type-check.
